title: Cons / I don't like — relationship-critique list (likely Emily)
html:
TL;DR

  • A bullet-list of "Cons / I don't like" items written in the same week as the outpace polish note — same writing burst, very different surface.
  • The subject is unnamed but the items map cleanly onto the same person being evaluated in the March 24 misalignment doc (almost certainly Emily).
  • Lists ten value/lifestyle frictions: solo-1-on-1 hangs with single straight men, openness to her child engaging in furry dynamics beyond home play, body-hair preferences, viewing relationships "as restrictive of freedoms," low ambition, anti-capitalism rhetoric paired with constant shopping, school stress, depression risk (with concern about post-partum), low libido (attributed to SSRIs), embracing gray-hair regrowth in a "given up" way.
  • Sits 9 days before Emily flies into Austin for the March 13–25 visit — Yahir is privately keeping score even as he's planning the trip.
  • Pre-cursor data for the explicit alignment/misalignment doc three weeks later ("Alignments header is blank, only misalignments listed").

What this artifact is

This is a raw debit ledger — Yahir is writing down the frictions he's been mentally accumulating but hasn't externalized in conversation. The structure is one-sided (no Pros / I do like header) which itself tells the story: by March 4, the ledger isn't being actively rebalanced. It mirrors the structural asymmetry that becomes visible in 2026 03 24 alignment misalignment.

How it triangulates with the arc

  • Feb 14: high-warmth Valentine letter from Costa Rica.
  • Feb 21: first major doubt spiral ("shes fucking Riley while I wait on the call").
  • March 4 (this doc): cons list, no pros side.
  • March 6: itinerary for the visit.
  • March 24: explicit alignment/misalignment ledger, alignments side blank.
  • April 4: "my relationship with emily seems to probably be coming to a close."

The three-week interval from this cons list to the April 4 drowning entry is essentially the visible decay arc.

Privacy notes

  • Mentions a child — stays as "her child", no name given even in raw, no extension here.
  • Subject not named in raw — left unnamed in summary too. The triangulation to Emily is inferential from the surrounding arc but should not be hard-asserted; the raw doc itself never says her name.
  • "SSRIs" — substance/medication mention; preserved because it's inside Yahir's own evaluative reasoning, not a clinical disclosure.

Connections
